rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (6/10) and rest on the same underlying assessment: solid C++ background at National Instruments but a gap in industrial automation and specific domain technologies. The wording shifts (e.g., 'Limited recent C++ experience', mentioning MQTT/IoT) are ordinary run-to-run rephrasings of the same job-relevant concerns, not new penalties tied to the name. Nothing in the variant keys off the change from a Hungarian-sounding name to an Anglo one — no shift in age, origin, or institution credit. These differences fall within normal noise.
